name: barbell front squat
comment: Set the barbell on a rack at upper-chest height. Position the bar in front of the shoulders by crossing the arms and placing the hands over the bar, with the upper arms parallel to the floor. Unrack the bar. Squat down by pushing the hips back and allowing the knees to travel forward, keeping the back straight and knees pointing in line with the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel. Extend the knees and hips until the legs are straight. Return and repeat.

name: lying triceps press
comment: Lie on a bench with a narrow overhand grip on the barbell. Hold the bar above the forehead with the arms extended. Lower the bar by bending the elbows. As the bar approaches the head, draw the elbows slightly back to allow the bar to clear. Extend the arms, returning the elbows to their original position as the arms reach full extension. Repeat.

name: barbell deadlift
comment: Stand with feet flat beneath the bar. Hinge down and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand or mixed grip. Lift the bar by extending the hips and knees to full extension. Pull the shoulders back at the top of the lift if rounded. Return and repeat.

name: Romanian deadlift
comment: Take the barbell from a rack with a shoulder-width to wide overhand grip. Stand with the feet shoulder-width or narrower. Push the hips back and lower the bar by hinging at the hips, keeping the bar close to the front of the legs. Allow the knees to bend slightly as the bar descends. Continue lowering until the hamstrings are fully stretched, keeping the spine neutral throughout. Drive the hips forward to return to standing. Pull the shoulders back slightly if rounded. Repeat.

name: seated military press
comment: Take the barbell from the rack with a slightly wider than shoulder-width overhand grip. Position the bar at upper-chest height. Press the bar overhead until the arms are fully extended. Lower to the upper chest and repeat.

name: barbell curl
comment: Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand grip. Keeping the elbows tucked to the sides, raise the bar until the forearms are vertical. Lower until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: flat barbell bench press
comment: Lie back on a flat bench. Unrack the barbell over the upper chest using a wide overhand grip. Lower the bar to the chest. Press the bar upward until the arms are fully extended. Avoid gripping too wide, as this limits range of motion. Repeat.

name: close-grip bench press
comment: Lie on the bench and take the barbell from the rack with a shoulder-width grip. Lower the bar to the chest, keeping the elbows close to the body. Press the barbell back up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: barbell t-bar row
comment: Bend the knees slightly and hinge forward over the handles with a flat back. Grasp the handles with a shoulder-width to wide overhand grip. Pull the bar up to the torso. Return until the arms are fully extended and the shoulders are stretched downward. Repeat.

name: barbell lunge
comment: Take the barbell from the rack and position it across the upper back, grasping the bar to each side. Step forward into a lunge, landing on the heel then the ball of the foot. Lower the body by bending the front knee and hip until the rear knee is just above the floor. Drive through the front foot to return to standing. Alternate legs with each repetition.

name: standing military press
comment: Take the barbell from a rack, or clean it from the floor, with a slightly wider than shoulder-width overhand grip. Position the bar at the front of the shoulders. Press the bar overhead until the arms are fully extended. Lower to the front of the shoulders and repeat.

name: E-Z bar curl
comment: Grasp the E-Z bar at the inner angled grips with a shoulder-width underhand grip. Keeping the elbows tucked to the sides, raise the bar until the forearms are vertical. Lower until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: barbell row
comment: Bend the knees slightly and hinge forward with a flat back. Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip. Pull the bar to the upper waist. Return until the arms are fully extended and the shoulders are stretched downward. Repeat.

name: barbell squat
comment: Set the barbell on a rack at upper-chest height and position it low across the upper back. Grasp the bar to each side. Unrack the bar and stand with a wide stance. Squat by pushing the hips back and allowing the knees to travel slightly forward, keeping the back straight and knees pointing in line with the feet. Descend until the thighs are just past parallel to the floor. Extend the hips and knees until the legs are straight. Return and repeat.

name: barbell rear delt row
comment: Bend the knees slightly and hinge forward until the torso is approximately horizontal, maintaining a flat back. Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip. Keeping the upper arms perpendicular to the torso, pull the bar up towards the upper chest until the upper arms are just beyond horizontal. Return and repeat.

name: barbell shrug
comment: Stand holding the barbell with an overhand or mixed grip, hands shoulder-width or slightly wider. Elevate the shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: incline barbell bench press
comment: Lie back on an incline bench. Unrack the barbell over the upper chest using a wide overhand grip. Lower the bar to the chest. Press until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: dip (chest)
comment: Attach weight using a dip belt, or hold a dumbbell between the lower legs. Mount the wide dip bars with an oblique grip, arms straight and shoulders above the hands. Keep the hips and knees slightly bent. Lower the body by bending the arms and allowing the elbows to flare outward. When a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders, push back up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: ab roller
comment: Kneel on a mat and grasp the roller handles with the core braced. Slowly roll forward, keeping the hips in line with the shoulders and avoiding any sagging in the lower back. Roll out only as far as control allows, then pull back using the abdominal muscles to return. Repeat.

name: decline crunch
comment: Secure the feet under the foot pad and lie back on a decline bench. Hold a barbell at chest level with a shoulder-width or slightly wider overhand grip. Curl the upper torso forward by flexing at the waist, keeping the lower back in contact with the bench. Return until the shoulders touch the bench. Repeat.

name: flat bench lying leg raise
comment: Attach cable ankle straps to both ankles. Sit at the end of the bench facing a low pulley and connect the cable to the straps. Lie back on the bench until the cable is taut. Grasp the sides of the bench behind the head. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees until the thighs are past perpendicular to the torso, or until the hips begin to lift from the bench. Return until the hips and knees are extended. Repeat.

name: captain's chair leg raise
comment: Grasp the handles and stand on the foot bar with the knees slightly bent and the hips against the back pad. Keeping the back straight, raise the legs as high as possible by flexing at the hips. Return and repeat.

name: hanging leg raise
comment: Stand beneath a high bar or pull-up handles. If using added weight, place it between the ankles. Reach up and grasp the bar. Hang with the legs straight and feet clear of the floor. Raise the legs by flexing the hips and knees until the hips are fully flexed or the knees are well above hip height. Return until the hips and knees are extended. Repeat.

name: chin-up
comment: Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand grip. Pull the body up until the elbows are tucked to the sides. Lower until the arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: air bicycle
comment: Lie on the back with hands behind the head and knees bent. Lift the shoulders off the floor, draw one knee towards the chest, and extend the opposite leg. Simultaneously rotate the torso to bring the opposite elbow towards the raised knee. Alternate sides in a continuous pedalling motion.

name: dip (triceps)
comment: Attach weight using a dip belt, or hold a dumbbell between the lower legs. Mount the shoulder-width dip bars with the arms straight and shoulders above the hands. Keep the hips straight. Lower the body until a slight stretch is felt in the shoulders. Push back up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: pull-up
comment: Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip. Pull the body up until the chin clears the bar. Lower until the arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: assisted pull-up
comment: Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip and kneel on the padded assistance platform. Lower the body with the arms extended. Pull up until the neck reaches hand height. Lower until the arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: seated dumbbell press
comment: Hold the dumbbells at each side of the shoulders with the elbows below the wrists. Press the dumbbells overhead until the arms are fully extended. Lower to the sides of the shoulders and repeat.

name: seated triceps press
comment: Hold a single dumbbell overhead with both hands cupped under the inner plate (heart-shaped grip). With the elbows pointing up, lower the forearms behind the upper arms by bending the elbows. Extend the wrists at the bottom to prevent the dumbbell catching the back of the neck. Raise the dumbbell back overhead by extending the elbows. Return and repeat.

name: donkey calf raise
comment: Position the lower back and hips beneath the padded lever and place the forearms on the supports. Place the toes and balls of the feet on the calf block with the heels and arches hanging off. Straighten the knees. Raise the heels by extending the ankles as high as possible, then lower until the calves are fully stretched. Repeat.

name: hammer curl
comment: Hold two dumbbells at the sides with the palms facing in and the arms straight. Keeping the elbows tucked in, curl one dumbbell up until the forearm is vertical and the thumb faces the shoulder. Lower to the starting position and repeat with the opposite arm. Continue alternating.

name: dumbbell lunge
comment: Stand holding dumbbells at the sides. Step forward into a lunge, landing on the heel then the ball of the foot. Lower the body by bending the front knee and hip until the rear knee is just above the floor. Drive through the front foot to return to standing. Alternate legs with each repetition.

name: dumbbell side lateral raise
comment: Hold the dumbbells in front of the thighs with the elbows slightly bent. Lean slightly forward from the hips and knees. Raise the arms to the sides until the elbows reach shoulder height, keeping the elbows at or above wrist level throughout. Lower and repeat.

name: Arnold dumbbell press
comment: Hold two dumbbells in front of the shoulders with the palms facing the body and elbows under the wrists. Begin by rotating the elbows out to the sides, then continue pressing the dumbbells overhead as the palms rotate to face forward. Lower back to the starting position in reverse and repeat.

name: alternating dumbbell curl
comment: Hold two dumbbells at the sides with the palms facing in and the arms straight. Keeping the elbows tucked in, curl one dumbbell and rotate the forearm until it is vertical and the palm faces the shoulder. Lower to the starting position and repeat with the opposite arm. Continue alternating.

name: one-arm dumbbell row
comment: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a flat bench for support, with the opposite foot planted on the floor. Pick up the dumbbell with the free hand, keeping the back flat and roughly parallel to the floor. Let the shoulder drop towards the floor. Row the dumbbell up to the side of the torso, retracting the shoulder blade at the top. Lower until the arm is fully extended and the shoulder is stretched downward. Repeat, then switch sides.

name: bent-over dumbbell rear delt raise
comment: Hold the dumbbells at each side. Bend the knees and hinge forward at the hips until the back is flat and close to horizontal. Position the elbows with a slight bend and the palms facing each other. Raise the arms to the sides until the elbows reach shoulder height, keeping the upper arms perpendicular to the torso throughout. Lead with the little finger side to maintain elbow height above the wrists. Lower and repeat.

name: seated dumbbell rear delt raise
comment: Sit on the edge of a bench with the feet planted slightly in front of the knees. Lean forward and rest the torso on the thighs. Grasp a dumbbell in each hand beneath the legs, with a slight bend in the elbows and the palms facing each other. Raise the arms to the sides until the elbows reach shoulder height, keeping the upper arms perpendicular to the torso throughout. Lead with the little finger side to maintain elbow height above the wrists. Lower and repeat.

name: flat dumbbell press
comment: Sit on a flat bench with the dumbbells resting on the thighs. Use the thighs to drive the weights up to shoulder height as you lie back. Position the dumbbells to the sides of the chest. Press the dumbbells up until the arms are fully extended. Lower to the sides of the chest until a slight stretch is felt. Repeat.

name: dumbbell front raise
comment: Hold the dumbbells in front of the thighs with the elbows straight or slightly bent. Raise both dumbbells forward and upward until the upper arms are above horizontal. Lower under control and repeat.

name: one-arm side lateral raise
comment: Attach a stirrup to a low cable pulley. Stand with the non-working side facing the pulley, holding the attachment in the hand furthest from the machine. Grasp a fixed support with the other hand if available. With a slight bend in the elbow, raise the working arm out to the side until the elbow reaches shoulder height. Lower and repeat, then switch sides.

name: incline dumbbell press
comment: Sit on an incline bench with the dumbbells resting on the thighs. Use the thighs to drive the weights up to shoulder height as you lean back. Position the dumbbells to the sides of the upper chest. Press the dumbbells up until the arms are fully extended. Lower to the sides of the chest until a slight stretch is felt. Repeat.

name: cable crunch
comment: Kneel below a high pulley. Grasp the rope attachment with both hands and place the wrists against the forehead. Push the hips back and allow the cable to extend the torso into a slight arch. With the hips stationary, curl the torso down by flexing the waist until the elbows travel towards the thighs. Return and repeat.

name: leg curl
comment: Lie face down on the bench with the knees just beyond the edge and the lower legs positioned under the lever pads. Grasp the handles. Curl the legs up by flexing the knees until the pads reach the back of the thighs. Lower until the knees are straight. Repeat.

name: machine t-bar row
comment: Bend the knees slightly and hinge forward over the handles with a flat back. Grasp the handles with a shoulder-width to wide overhand grip. Pull the bar up to the torso. Return until the arms are fully extended and the shoulders are stretched downward. Repeat.

name: wide-grip front-lat pulldown
comment: Grasp the cable bar with a wide grip. Sit with the thighs secured under the supports. Pull the bar down to the upper chest. Return until the arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: triceps pushdown
comment: Stand facing the high pulley and grasp the cable attachment with a narrow overhand grip. Pin the elbows to the sides. Extend the arms downward until fully straight. Return until the forearms are close to the upper arms. Repeat.

name: face pull
comment: Set the cable machine with a rope attachment at approximately eye height. Grasp the rope with an overhand or neutral grip. Pull the rope towards the face, flaring the elbows high and out to the sides and separating the hands at the end of the movement. Return under control and repeat.

name: leg press
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded support. Place the feet slightly high on the platform. Extend the hips and knees, then release the dock lever and grasp the handles. Bend the hips and knees to lower the platform until the hips are fully flexed. Push the platform back up by extending the knees and hips. Repeat.

name: calf press
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded support. Extend the hips and knees and grasp the handles to the sides. Place the toes and balls of the feet on the lower portion of the platform with the heels and arches hanging off. Push through the toes by extending the ankles as far as possible. Return by bending the ankles until the calves are fully stretched. Repeat.

name: standing calf raise
comment: Position the shoulders under the padded lever. Place the toes and balls of the feet on the calf block with the heels and arches hanging off. Grasp the handles or sides of the lever. Stand upright by extending the hips and knees. Raise the heels by extending the ankles as high as possible, then lower until the calves are fully stretched. Repeat.

name: seated calf raise
comment: Sit on the seat facing the lever. Pull the hand lever towards the body to release it. Place the balls of the feet on the platform with the heels hanging off, then position the lower thighs under the lever pads. Push the hand lever away to engage. Place the hands on top of the thigh pads. Raise the heels by extending the ankles as high as possible, then lower until the calves are fully stretched. Repeat.

name: close-grip front-lat pulldown
comment: Grasp the parallel cable attachment. Sit with the thighs secured under the supports. Pull the attachment down to the upper chest. Return until the arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: seated cable row
comment: Sit at the cable machine and grasp the attachment. Place the feet on the footrest and slide the hips back with a slight bend in the knees. Pull the attachment to the waist while keeping the lower back straight. Draw the shoulders back and push the chest forward. Return until the arms are extended and the shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: hack squat
comment: Step onto the platform and position the back against the padded support with the shoulders under the shoulder pads. Place the feet high on the platform. Extend the hips and knees, then release the dock levers. Lower by bending the hips and knees until the hips are fully flexed. Push back up by extending the knees and hips. Repeat.

name: dumbbell chest fly
comment: Lie back on a flat bench holding two dumbbells above the chest with the arms fixed in a slightly bent position. Internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point out to the sides. Lower the dumbbells to the sides until the chest muscles are fully stretched. Bring the dumbbells back together in a wide arc until they are nearly touching. Repeat.

name: cable chest fly
comment: Stand between two cable stations with the pulleys set at chest height. Grasp a stirrup in each hand and adopt a slight split stance for balance. With a fixed, slight bend in the elbows, bring the handles together in a wide arc in front of the chest. Return until a slight stretch is felt in the chest. Repeat.

name: pec deck
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the pad. If a foot lever is available, press it to move the padded arms forward. Place the forearms on the padded levers with the upper arms approximately parallel to the floor. Release the foot lever. Push the levers together until they meet in front of the chest. Return until the chest muscles are fully stretched. Repeat.

name: decline barbell bench press
comment: Lie back on a decline bench with the feet secured under the leg brace. Unrack the barbell over the chest using a wide overhand grip. Lower the bar to the chest. Press until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: push-up
comment: Start face down on the floor with the hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Press up by extending the arms, keeping the body in a straight line. If adding weight, have a partner place plates on the upper back. Lower the body by bending the arms until the chest is close to the floor. Press back up until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: upright row
comment: Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width or slightly narrower overhand grip. Pull the bar up towards the chin with the elbows leading. Allow the wrists to flex as the bar rises. Lower and repeat.

name: cable lateral raise
comment: Set two low cable pulleys on opposite sides. Grasp the left stirrup with the right hand and the right stirrup with the left hand, crossing the cables in front of the body. Stand upright. With the elbows slightly bent, raise both arms out to the sides until the elbows reach shoulder height. Lower and repeat.

name: machine shoulder press
comment: Sit on the machine and grasp the lever handles to each side with an overhand grip. Press the handles upward until the arms are fully extended overhead. Lower and repeat.

name: reverse pec deck
aliases: lever isolateral seated reverse fly (parallel grip)
comment: Sit on the machine facing the pad with the chest against it. Grasp the parallel handles with the thumbs up at shoulder height. Bend the elbows slightly and keep them level with the shoulders. Pull the handles apart and back until the elbows are just behind the back. Return and repeat.

name: inverted row
comment: Lie on the back beneath a fixed horizontal bar. Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip and place the heels on an elevated surface. Keeping the body straight, pull the chest up to the bar. Return until the arms are extended and the shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: step-up
comment: Stand holding dumbbells at the sides, facing a bench. Place one foot on the bench. Drive through that foot to step up, bringing the trailing foot alongside. Step back down with the trailing foot first. Alternate the leading leg with each repetition.

name: leg extension
comment: Sit on the machine with the back against the padded support. Place the front of the lower legs under the padded lever, aligning the knees with the lever's pivot point. Grasp the handles for support. Extend the knees to raise the lever until the legs are straight. Return by bending the knees. Repeat.

name: glute bridge
comment: Lie on the back on a mat with the knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Brace the core and press the lower back into the mat. Squeeze the glutes and drive through the heels to lift the hips until the knees, hips, and shoulders form a straight line. Hold briefly at the top, then lower the hips back towards the mat without fully resting. Repeat.

name: barbell hip thrust
comment: Sit on the floor with a bench behind the back. Roll the barbell over the hips and rest the upper back against the edge of the bench. Place the feet flat on the floor, approximately shoulder-width apart, with knees bent. Grasp the bar to each side. Drive the hips upward by extending them until the body is straight. Lower and repeat.

name: hip thrust
comment: Sit on the floor with a bench behind the back. Position the weight over the hips and rest the upper back against the edge of the bench. Place the feet flat on the floor, approximately shoulder-width apart, with knees bent. Drive the hips upward by extending them until the body is straight. Lower and repeat.

name: barbell sumo deadlift
comment: Position the feet under the bar with a very wide stance, toes pointed out. Squat down and grasp the bar between the legs with a shoulder-width mixed grip, keeping the arms straight, chest high, and back flat. Drive the feet out into the floor and pull the chest up to lift the bar. As the bar passes the knees, extend the knees and drive the hips through. At the top, pull the shoulders back. Return the bar to the floor by pushing the hips back and the knees outward. Repeat.

name: stiff-leg deadlift
comment: Stand on a low platform with feet shoulder-width or narrower, positioned over the bar. Hinge forward with the knees nearly locked and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width overhand or mixed grip. Lift to a standing position. Lower the bar towards the top of the feet by hinging at the hips, bending the knees only slightly. Pull the shoulders back if rounded. Repeat.

name: walking lunge
comment: Stand holding dumbbells at the sides. Step forward into a lunge, landing on the heel then the ball of the foot. Lower the body until the rear knee is just above the floor. Drive through the front foot and bring the rear foot forward into the next lunge. Continue walking forward, alternating legs.

name: preacher curl
comment: Sit at the preacher bench and rest the back of the upper arms on the pad. Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width underhand grip. Curl the bar up until the forearms are vertical. Lower until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: concentration curl
comment: Sit on a bench with the feet planted wider than hip-width. Hold a dumbbell in one hand and rest the back of that upper arm against the inner thigh on the same side. Curl the dumbbell up towards the shoulder. Lower until the arm is fully extended. Repeat, then switch arms.

name: cable curl
comment: Grasp the low pulley cable bar with a shoulder-width underhand grip. Stand close to the pulley. Keeping the elbows tucked to the sides, raise the bar until the forearms are vertical. Lower until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: dumbbell prone incline curl
comment: Lie face down on an incline bench with the shoulders near the top. Rest the knees on the seat or straddle the legs to the sides. Grasp the dumbbells with an underhand grip. Curl the dumbbells up until the arms are fully flexed. Lower until the arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: skullcrusher
comment: Lie on a bench with a narrow overhand grip on the E-Z bar. Hold the bar above the forehead with the arms extended. Lower the bar by bending the elbows. As the bar approaches the head, draw the elbows slightly back to allow the bar to clear. Extend the arms, returning the elbows to their original position as the arms reach full extension. Repeat.

name: overhead triceps extension
comment: Hold a single dumbbell overhead with both hands cupped under the inner plate (heart-shaped grip). With the elbows pointing up, lower the forearms behind the upper arms by bending the elbows. Extend the wrists at the bottom to prevent the dumbbell catching the back of the neck. Raise the dumbbell back overhead by extending the elbows. Return and repeat.

name: cable overhead triceps extension
comment: Stand or sit facing away from the cable machine. Grasp the bar or rope attachment with a narrow overhand grip and position it behind the head, with the elbows pointing up. Extend the arms overhead until fully straight. Lower back behind the head by bending the elbows, allowing the forearms to travel behind the upper arms. Repeat.

name: bench dip
comment: Sit on the edge of a bench with a second bench placed parallel at leg's length in front. Place the hands on the edge of the bench behind, straighten the arms, and position the heels on the second bench with the legs straight. If adding weight, have a partner place a plate on the lap. Lower the body by bending the arms until a slight stretch is felt in the chest or shoulders. Push back up and repeat.

name: plank
comment: Lie face down on a mat and place the forearms flat with the elbows directly under the shoulders. Press up onto the forearms and toes, keeping the body in a straight line from head to heels. Brace the core and hold the position.

name: side plank
comment: Lie on the side on a mat. Place the forearm on the mat directly beneath the shoulder, perpendicular to the body. Stack the upper leg on top of the lower and straighten both. Raise the hips by lifting the waist until the body forms a straight line. Hold the position, then lower and repeat on the opposite side.

name: Russian twist
comment: Sit on the floor with the hips and knees bent. Hold a weight with both hands and lean back slightly, balancing on the hips. Rotate the torso and reach the weight towards the floor to one side. Return through the centre and reach to the opposite side. Continue alternating in a controlled motion.

name: mountain climber
comment: Start in a high press-up position with the hands shoulder-width apart and the body in a straight line. Keeping the core braced, drive one knee towards the chest, then quickly return it and drive the opposite knee in. Continue alternating legs in a controlled, rhythmic motion.

name: hanging knee raise
comment: Reach up and grasp a high bar or pull-up handles. Hang with the legs straight and feet clear of the floor. Raise the knees towards the chest by flexing the hips and knees simultaneously. Return until the legs are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable neck flexion
comment: Sit on a bench facing away from a mid-height cable pulley. Fit the cable neck harness attachment. Rest the forearms on the thighs for support. Draw the head forward by flexing the neck until the chin reaches the upper chest. Return by extending the neck and repeat.

name: lever neck flexion (plate loaded)
comment: Sit in the machine and position the padded lever against the forehead, aligning the neck with the lever axis. Grasp the handles for support. Flex the neck forward until the chin reaches the upper chest. Return by extending the neck and repeat.

name: lever neck flexion
comment: Sit in the machine and position the padded lever against the forehead, aligning the neck with the lever axis. Grasp the handles for support. Flex the neck forward until the chin reaches the upper chest. Return by extending the neck and repeat.

name: weighted neck flexion
comment: Place a folded towel on a weight plate for cushioning. Lie across a bench with the upper back supported and the hips off the edge, feet flat on the floor. Hold the plate against the forehead with the towel between. Lift the head by flexing the neck until the chin reaches the upper chest. Return by lowering the head and repeat.

name: cable neck extension
comment: Sit on a bench facing the cable pulley. Fit the cable neck harness attachment. Rest the forearms on the thighs for support. Extend the neck backward, moving the head away from the pulley. Return by bending the neck forward until the chin reaches the upper chest. Repeat.

name: lever neck extension (plate loaded)
comment: Sit in the machine and position the back of the head against the padded lever, aligning the neck with the lever axis. Grasp the handles for support. Extend the neck to move the head back as far as possible. Return by bending the neck forward until the chin reaches the upper chest. Repeat.

name: lever neck extension
comment: Sit in the machine and position the back of the head against the padded lever, aligning the neck with the lever axis. Grasp the handles for support. Extend the neck to move the head back as far as possible. Return by bending the neck forward until the chin reaches the upper chest. Repeat.

name: weighted seated neck extension
comment: Place a folded towel on a weight plate for cushioning. Sit on a bench or stool and lean forward with the torso resting on the thighs. Hold the plate against the back of the head with the towel between. Extend the neck to raise the head as far back as possible. Return by bending the neck until the chin is towards the chest. Repeat.

name: weighted neck harness extension
comment: Sit on a bench and put on the weighted neck harness. Lean forward so the weight hangs freely in front. Extend the neck to raise the head as far back as possible. Return by bending the neck until the chin is towards the chest. Repeat.

name: band resistive neck retraction
comment: Anchor a resistance band across a rack at head height. Stand with the back of the neck against the band and the arms positioned to each side. Grasp a fixed bar above if within reach. Retract the chin by pulling the head straight back, flattening the cervical curve without tilting the head up or down. Hold briefly, then release and repeat.

name: lying neck retraction isometric
comment: Lie on the back on a mat. Gently press the back of the head into the mat. Hold the position briefly, then release and repeat.

name: barbell front raise
comment: Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, with the elbows straight or slightly bent. Raise the barbell forward and upward until the upper arms are above horizontal. Lower and repeat.

name: cable isolateral seated front raise
comment: Sit on the seat above the twin cable pulleys. Grasp the stirrups on each side. Sit upright with the arms hanging straight down to each side, palms facing back. Raise the stirrups forward and upward until the upper arms are well above horizontal. Lower and repeat.

name: cable alternating isolateral front raise
comment: Stand facing away from two low cable pulleys with a stirrup in each hand. Step forward slightly so the arms hang back at the sides, elbows straight or slightly bent. Raise one arm forward and upward until well above horizontal. Lower and repeat with the opposite arm, alternating throughout.

name: cable one arm front raise
comment: Attach a stirrup to a low cable pulley. Stand facing away from the pulley with the arm back at the side, elbow straight or slightly bent. Raise the arm forward and upward until well above horizontal. Lower and repeat. Switch arms and continue.

name: cable isolateral standing shoulder press
comment: Stand between two low to mid-height cable pulleys. Grasp the stirrups on each side and position them at shoulder height with the elbows down and hands just above or slightly inside the elbows. Press the stirrups overhead until the arms are fully extended. Return to the sides of the shoulders and repeat.

name: cable isolateral shoulder press
comment: Sit on a seat and grasp the stirrups on each side from a low position. Position the stirrups at shoulder height with the elbows down and hands just above or slightly inside the elbows. Press the stirrups overhead until the arms are fully extended. Return to the sides of the shoulders and repeat.

name: dumbbell arnold press
comment: Hold two dumbbells in front of the shoulders with the palms facing the body and elbows under the wrists. Begin by rotating the elbows out to the sides, then continue pressing the dumbbells overhead as the palms rotate to face forward. Lower back to the starting position in reverse and repeat.

name: dumbbell alternating front raise
comment: Hold the dumbbells in front of the thighs with the elbows straight or slightly bent. Raise one dumbbell forward and upward, palm facing down, until the upper arm is above horizontal. Lower and repeat with the opposite arm. Continue alternating.

name: dumbbell one arm shoulder press
comment: Hold a dumbbell at the shoulder with the elbow below the wrist. Press the dumbbell overhead until the arm is fully extended. Lower to the side of the shoulder and repeat.

name: lever isolateral military press (plate loaded)
comment: Sit in the machine and grasp the lever handles on each side with an overhand grip. Press the handles upward until the arms are fully extended. Lower and repeat.

name: lever reclined shoulder press (plate loaded)
comment: Lie back on the padded support facing upward. Grasp the lever handles on each side with an overhand grip. Press until the arms are fully extended. Lower and repeat.

name: lever isolateral shoulder press (plate loaded)
comment: Sit in the machine and grasp the lever handles on each side with an overhand grip. Press the handles overhead until the arms are fully extended. Lower and repeat.

name: lever reclined shoulder press
comment: Lie back on the padded support facing upward. Grasp the lever handles on each side with an overhand grip. Press until the arms are fully extended. Lower and repeat.

name: lever shoulder press (parallel grip)
comment: Sit on the machine and grasp the parallel bar handles at shoulder height on each side. Press the levers overhead until the arms are fully extended. Lower and repeat.

name: sled shoulder press
comment: Grasp the lever handles on each side with an overhand grip. Press the handles overhead until the arms are fully extended. Lower and repeat.

name: smith shoulder press
comment: Sit on a bench with the bar positioned in front of the shoulders. Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip. Disengage by raising and rotating the bar back. Press the bar overhead until the arms are fully extended. Lower to the front of the shoulders and repeat.

name: suspended front raise
comment: Grasp the suspension handles and step back until the arms are fully extended in front. Keeping the arms straight and shoulders back, step forward to recline the body to the desired angle. With the palms facing down and body straight, raise the arms overhead by flexing at the shoulders. Return to the starting position and repeat.

name: cable upright row
comment: Grasp the cable bar with a shoulder-width or slightly narrower overhand grip. Stand close to the pulley. Pull the bar up towards the chin with the elbows leading. Allow the wrists to flex as the bar rises. Lower and repeat.

name: cable one arm upright row
comment: Attach a stirrup to a low cable pulley. Stand sideways to the machine with the working arm on the far side. Grasp the stirrup with an overhand grip. Place the other hand on the machine column or a fixed support for stability. Pull the stirrup up towards the front of the shoulder with the elbow leading. Allow the wrist to flex as it rises. Lower and repeat, then switch sides.

name: cable upright row (with rope attachment)
comment: Grasp the rope close to each end with an overhand grip. Stand close to the pulley. Pull the rope up towards the front of the shoulders with the elbows leading. Allow the wrists to flex as the rope rises. Lower and repeat.

name: cable isolateral y raise
comment: Stand between two low cable pulleys set medium-width apart. Grasp the left stirrup with the right hand and the right stirrup with the left, crossing the cables in front of the hips. Step back slightly and stand upright. With the elbows slightly bent, raise both arms upward and out to the sides in a Y shape until the hands are above head height. Lower in the reverse pattern and repeat.

name: dumbbell incline lateral raise
comment: Grasp a dumbbell in one hand. Lie on a 30°–45° incline bench on the opposite side, with the top arm hanging down and the dumbbell in front of the lower thigh. Raise the dumbbell until the upper arm is perpendicular to the torso, maintaining a slight fixed bend in the elbow throughout. Lower and repeat.

name: dumbbell lying lateral raise
comment: Lie on the side with the legs slightly separated for support. Grasp a dumbbell in front of the thigh. Raise the dumbbell until the arm is vertical, maintaining a fixed elbow position (10°–30° bend) throughout. Lower and repeat.

name: dumbbell upright row
comment: Grasp the dumbbells and stand with the palms facing the front of the thighs. Pull the dumbbells up towards the front of the shoulders with the elbows leading out to the sides. Allow the wrists to flex as the dumbbells rise. Lower and repeat.

name: dumbbell one arm upright row
comment: Hold a dumbbell with the palm facing the front of the thigh. Use the free hand to steady against a fixed support. Pull the dumbbell up towards the front of the shoulder with the elbow leading. Allow the wrist to flex as the dumbbell rises. Lower and repeat, then switch arms.

name: lever isolateral lateral raise (plate loaded)
comment: Sit in the machine. Position the bent arms between the padded lever arms and the sides of the body. Raise the arms to the sides until the upper arms are horizontal. Return and repeat.

name: lever isolateral lateral raise
comment: Sit in the machine. Position the bent arms between the padded lever arms and the sides of the body. Grasp the handles if available. Raise the arms to the sides until the upper arms are horizontal. Return and repeat.

name: smith upright row
comment: Stand facing the bar set at mid-thigh height. Grasp the bar with a shoulder-width or slightly narrower overhand grip. Disengage by rotating the bar back and standing upright. Pull the bar up towards the chin with the elbows leading. Allow the wrists to flex as the bar rises. Lower and repeat.

name: suspended y lateral raise
comment: Grasp the suspension handles and step back, then lean forward to recline the body to the desired angle with the arms extended in front and palms angled inward. Keeping the arms straight, raise them upward and outward into a Y shape. Return and repeat.

name: cable isolateral rear lateral raise
comment: Stand between two cable columns. Grasp the left stirrup with the right hand and the right stirrup with the left. Bend the knees and hinge forward at the hips until the torso is approximately horizontal, keeping the back flat. With the elbows slightly bent and pointing outward, raise the arms to the sides until the elbows reach shoulder height, keeping the upper arms perpendicular to the torso. Lower and repeat.

name: cable isolateral reverse fly
comment: Stand facing two cable pulleys set close together at approximately shoulder height. Grasp a stirrup in each hand and step back until the cables are taut. Adopt a staggered stance. With the elbows pointing out and arms straight or slightly bent, pull the stirrups out to the sides. Maintain the elbow position throughout. Return and repeat.

name: dumbbell rear delt row
comment: Kneel over the side of a bench, supporting the body with one hand and the same-side knee on the bench. Grasp a dumbbell in the free hand. Pull the dumbbell out and up to the side, keeping the upper arm perpendicular to the torso, until the upper arm is just beyond horizontal. Lower and repeat.

name: lever isolateral lying rear lateral raise (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral lying rear lateral raise (plate loaded)
comment: Lie face down on the machine with the chest against the padding. Position the arms between the padded lever arms with the elbows below the shoulders. Pull the arms up and out until the elbows are beyond the level of the back. Return and repeat.

name: lever isolateral rear delt row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral rear delt row (plate loaded)
comment: Set the seat to a low position. Sit with the chest against the pad and grasp the upper handles. Pull the lever with the elbows flaring out to the sides until the upper arms are just beyond parallel to the floor, keeping them horizontal and perpendicular to the torso. Return and repeat.

name: lever isolateral lying rear lateral raise
aliases: lever isolateral lying rear lateral raise
comment: Lie face down on the machine with the chest against the padding. Position the arms between the padded lever arms with the elbows below the shoulders. Pull the arms up and out until the elbows are beyond the level of the back. Lower and repeat.

name: lever seated rear delt row
comment: Set the seat to a low position. Sit with the chest against the pad and grasp the upper handles. Pull the lever with the elbows flaring out to the sides until the upper arms are just beyond parallel to the floor, keeping them horizontal and perpendicular to the torso. Return and repeat.

name: lever isolateral seated reverse fly (pronated parallel grip)
aliases: lever isolateral seated reverse fly (pronated parallel grip)
comment: Sit in the machine with the chest against the pad. Grasp the parallel handles from the inside with the thumbs pointing down at shoulder height. Keeping the elbows elevated, pull the handles apart and back until the elbows are just behind the back. Return and repeat.

name: smith rear delt row
comment: Set the Smith bar to a low height. Bend the knees and hinge forward with a flat back. Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip. Disengage by rotating the bar back. Keeping the upper arms perpendicular to the torso, pull the bar up towards the upper chest until the upper arms are just beyond parallel to the floor. Return and repeat.

name: suspended reverse fly
comment: Grasp the suspension handles and step back, then lean forward to recline the body to the desired angle with the arms extended in front. Internally rotate the shoulders so the elbows point outward, keeping the arms straight or slightly bent. Pull the handles out to the sides in the plane of the shoulders, maintaining a fixed elbow position, until the arms are in line with one another. Return and repeat.

name: cable front lateral raise
comment: Attach a stirrup to a low cable pulley. Stand with the non-working side facing the pulley. Grasp a fixed support with the other hand if available. With the elbow straight or slightly bent, raise the working arm out to the side at approximately 30° in front until it reaches shoulder height. Lower and repeat, then switch sides.

name: dumbbell front lateral raise
comment: Hold a dumbbell in front of the thigh with the arm straight. Rotate the little finger side outward. Raise the arm to the side and slightly forward until shoulder height or slightly higher. Lower and repeat, then switch arms.

name: dumbbell full can lateral raise
comment: Hold the dumbbells with the palms facing forward. Raise both arms out to the sides with the thumb side up. Lower and repeat.

name: machine-assisted triceps dip
comment: Mount the shoulder-width dip bars with the arms straight and shoulders above the hands. Step down onto the assistance lever. Keep the hips and knees straight. Lower the body until a slight stretch is felt in the shoulders. Push back up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: machine-assisted triceps dip (kneeling)
comment: Mount the shoulder-width dip bars with the arms straight and shoulders above the hands. Kneel on the padded assistance platform with the hips slightly bent. Lower the body until a slight stretch is felt in the shoulders. Push back up until the arms are straight. Repeat.

name: barbell lying triceps extension "skull crusher"
comment: Lie on a bench with a narrow overhand grip on the barbell. Hold the bar above the forehead with the arms extended. Lower the bar to the forehead by bending the elbows. Extend the arms and repeat.

name: barbell triceps extension
comment: Sit on a bench holding the barbell overhead with a narrow overhand grip. Lower the barbell behind the head by bending the elbows, allowing the forearms to travel behind the upper arms while the elbows remain pointing up. Extend the elbows to raise the barbell back overhead until the arms are straight and vertical. Repeat.

name: cable bent-over triceps extension
comment: Grasp the cable bar from a medium-high pulley with a narrow or shoulder-width overhand grip. Turn away from the pulley and position the bar behind the neck. Hinge forward and adopt a split stance. Allow the elbows to be drawn back by the cable tension. Extend the forearms forward until the elbows are straight. Return the bar over the neck and repeat.

name: cable lying triceps extension
comment: Lie on a bench and grasp the cable bar with a narrow overhand grip. Hold the bar above the face with the arms extended. Lower the bar by bending the elbows. As the bar approaches the head, draw the elbows slightly back to allow it to clear. Extend the arms, returning the elbows to their original position. Repeat.

name: cable one arm pushdown
comment: Attach a stirrup to a high cable pulley. Grasp the stirrup with an underhand grip and pin the elbow to the side. Extend the arm downward until fully straight. Return until the forearm is close to the upper arm. Repeat, then switch arms.

name: cable pushdown (with back support)
comment: Place back against vertical back support. Grasp cable attachment from high pulley with narrow overhand grip. Position elbows to side. Extend arms down. Return until forearm is close to upper arm. Repeat.

name: cable pushdown (with v-bar)
comment: Face high pulley and grasp V-bar attachment with narrow overhand grip. Position elbows to side. Extend arms down. Return until forearm is close to upper arm. Repeat.

name: cable triceps dip
comment: Step between shoulder width dip bars with dip belt around waist. Kneel as close to low pulley and attach cable to dip belt. Stand up and mount dip bar, arms straight with shoulders above hands. Keep hips straight. Lower body by bending arms until slight stretch is felt in shoulders. Push body up until arms are straight. Repeat.

name: cable triceps extension (with rope)
comment: From low pulley cable, grasp ends of rope attachment just under enlarged ends. Raise one arm over head while turning body away from pulley. Face away from pulley with feet staggered. Position ends of ropes behind head or neck and elbows upward over head. Raise ends of rope overhead by extending forearms until arms are straight. Lower rope attachment until forearms are against upper arms. Repeat.

name: dumbbell kickback
comment: Kneel over bench with arm supporting body. Grasp dumbbell. Position upper arm parallel to floor. Extend arm until it is straight. Return and repeat. Continue with opposite arm.

name: dumbbell lying triceps extension
comment: Lie on bench and position dumbbells over head with arms extended. Lower dumbbells by bending elbow until they are to sides of head. Extend arm. Repeat.

name: dumbbell one arm triceps extension (on bench)
aliases: dumbbell one arm triceps extension (on bench)
comment: Position dumbbell behind neck with elbow positioned upward. Extend arm until straight while maintaining upper arm's vertical position throughout exercise. Return until dumbbell is behind neck or shoulder and repeat. Continue with opposite arm.

name: lever isolateral close grip bench press (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral close grip bench press (plate loaded)
comment: Lie on bench with lever handles above sides of chest. Grasp handles with narrow overhand grip just above each side of chest. Press lever up until arms are extended. Return weights down to starting position. Repeat.

name: lever triceps dip (plate loaded)
comment: Sit upright on seat. If possible, place handles in narrow position. Grasp handles. Push levers down with elbows pointing back. Allow lever bar to raise until shoulders are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever triceps extension (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat. Place forearms on lever pad and back of upper arms, parallel on padding with elbows in line with lever's fulcrum. Push lever down until arms are fully extended. Return and repeat.

name: lever triceps dip
comment: If possible, place handles in narrow position. Sit on seat with back against pad. Grasp handles and position elbows back. Push levers down by straightening arms downward. Allow lever bar to raise with elbows pointing back until shoulders are slightly stretched. Repeat.

name: lever triceps extension
comment: Sit on seat. Grasp handles and place back of upper arms parallel on padding with elbows approximately in line with lever's fulcrum. Push lever down until arms are fully extended. Return until forearms contact upper arms. Repeat.

name: lever triceps extension (with preacher pad)
comment: Sit on seat. Grasp handles and place back of upper arms parallel on padding with elbows approximately in line with lever's fulcrum. Push lever down until arms are fully extended. Return until forearms contact upper arms. Repeat.

name: smith close grip bench press
comment: Lie on bench with bar above chest and grasp bar with shoulder width or slightly narrower grip. Disengage bar by rotating bar back. Lower weight to chest with elbows close to body. Push bar back up until arms are straight. Repeat.

name: suspended triceps extension
comment: Grasp handles and step forward between suspension trainers. Position arms downward and slightly forward, nearly parallel with suspension straps. Lean forward, placing upper body weight onto handles with arms straight, while stepping back onto forefeet so body is leaning forward at desired angle. Straighten body, so torso is in-line with legs. Lower body by bending elbows, allowing head to travel between suspension handles. Raise body up and back up until arms are extended. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral curl
comment: Stand between or facing double low pulleys. Grasp stirrups to each side, palms facing forward. Pull stirrups forward and upward toward shoulders while keeping elbows stationary. Return until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable alternating isolateral curl
comment: Stand between or facing double low pulleys. Grasp stirrups to each side, palms facing forward. Pull one stirrup up forward and upward toward shoulder while keeping elbow stationary. Return until arm is fully extended. Repeat on other arm, alternating between sides.

name: cable one arm curl
comment: Face low pulley and grasp stirrup cable attachment to one side with underhand grip. With elbow to side, raise bar until forearms are vertical. Lower until arms are fully extended. Repeat. Continue with opposite arm.

name: dumbbell incline curl
comment: Sit back on 45-60 degree incline bench. With arms hanging down straight, position two dumbbells with palms facing inward. With elbows back to sides, raise one dumbbell and rotate forearm until forearm is vertical and palm faces shoulder. Lower to original position and repeat with opposite arm. Continue to alternate between sides.

name: lever isolateral curl (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat and grasp handles with underhand grip. Raise lever handles until elbows are fully flexed with back of upper arm remaining on pad. Lower handles until arm is fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral curl
comment: Sit on seat and grasp handles with underhand grip. Raise lever handles until elbows are fully flexed with back of upper arm remaining on pad. Lower handles until arm is fully extended. Repeat.

name: suspended arm curl
comment: Grasp suspension handles and momentarily step back until arms are extended forward and straight. While keeping arms straight and shoulders back, step forward so body is reclined back. Position palms up or slightly inward. Keeping body straight, bring handles toward top of shoulders by flexing arms, while keeping elbows pointed forward. Return by straightening arms and repeat.

name: barbell prone incline curl
comment: Lie prone on incline bench with shoulders near top of incline. Knees can rest on seat or legs can be straddled to sides. From low rack or partner, grasp curl bar with shoulder width underhand grip. Raise bar until arms are flexed. Lower barbell until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable concentration curl
comment: Facing low pulley cable, sit on seat or bench with legs apart to each side. Grasp stirrup attachment. Place upper arm against inner thigh. Pull stirrup to front of shoulder until elbow is completely flexed. Lower stirrup until arm is fully extended. Repeat. Continue with opposite arm.

name: cable preacher curl
comment: Sit on preacher bench placing back of arms on pad. Grasp cable bar with shoulder width underhand grip. Raise cable bar toward shoulders. Lower cable bar until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable isolateral preacher curl
comment: Grasp cable stirrups with each hand. Sit on preacher bench placing back of arms on pad with palms up. Raise both stirrups upward toward shoulders. Lower stirrups until arm is fully extended. Repeat.

name: dumbbell preacher curl
comment: Grasp dumbbell and sit on preacher bench. With arm bent and palm facing shoulder, place back of arm down on pad. Lower dumbbell until arm is fully extended. Raise dumbbell until forearm is vertical. Repeat. Continue with opposite arm.

name: lever isolateral preacher curl (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on curl machine placing back of arms on pad. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Align elbows near same pivot point as fulcrum of lever. Raise lever handles toward shoulders. Lower handles until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral preacher curl (arms high, plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral preacher curl (arms high, plate loaded)
comment: Sit on curl machine. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Align elbows near same pivot point as fulcrum of lever while placing back of arms up on pads. Pull lever handles toward shoulders. Return handles until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever preacher curl
comment: Sit on curl machine placing back of arms on pad. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Align elbows near same pivot point as fulcrum of lever. Raise lever handles toward shoulders. Lower handles until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral preacher curl (arms high)
comment: Sit on curl machine. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Align elbows near same pivot point as fulcrum of lever while placing back of arms up on pads. Pull lever handles toward shoulders. Return handles until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: barbell reverse curl
comment: Grasp bar with shoulder width overhand grip. With elbows to side, raise bar until forearms are vertical. Lower until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable hammer curl
comment: Grasp cable rope with palms facing inward. Stand upright with arms straight down to sides. With elbows to side, raise rope forward and upward with both arms until forearms are vertical. Lower until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable reverse curl
comment: Grasp cable bar with shoulder width overhand grip. With elbows to side, raise bar until forearms are vertical. Lower until arms are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral hammer preacher curl (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral hammer preacher curl (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on curl machine placing back of arms on pad. Grasp lever handles and position grips with thumb side up. Align elbows near same pivot point as fulcrum of lever. Raise lever handles toward shoulders, maintaining palms in grip. Lower handles until arm is fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral reverse curl
comment: Sit on seat and grasp handles with overhand grip. Position elbows to sides so they are in line with lever's fulcrum. Raise lever handles until elbows are fully flexed with back of upper arm remaining on pad. Lower handles until arm is fully extended. Repeat.

name: barbell wrist curl
comment: Sit and grasp bar with narrow to shoulder width underhand grip. Rest forearms on thighs with wrists just beyond knees. Allow barbell to roll out of palms down to fingers. Raise barbell back up by gripping and pointing knuckles up as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: cable wrist curl
comment: Sit and grasp cable bar with narrow to shoulder width underhand grip. Rest forearms on thighs with wrists just beyond knees. Allow cable bar to roll out of palms down to fingers. Raise cable bar back up by gripping and pointing knuckles up as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: dumbbell wrist curl
comment: Sit and grasp dumbbell with underhand grip. Rest forearm on thigh with wrist just beyond knee. Allow dumbbell to roll out of palm down to fingers. Raise dumbbell back up by gripping and pointing knuckles up as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: lever grip (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat, straddling apparatus. Grasp lever handles on each side; fingers around lever handles and base of thumbs around stationary bars. Squeeze bars together with both hands until lower lever makes contact with upper bars. Lower to original position and repeat.

name: lever wrist curl (plate loaded)
comment: Grasp bar with underhand grip. Place forearm on padded platform. Raise bar up until wrist is fully flexed. Lower and repeat.

name: lever isolateral grip
comment: Sit on seat. Grasp lever handles on each side; fingers around lever handles and base of thumbs around stationary bars. Squeeze bars together with both hands until lever makes contact with stationary bars. Lower to original position and repeat.

name: lever isolateral wrist curl
comment: Grasp bar with underhand grip to each side. Place forearms on arm pads. Raise handles up until wrist is fully flexed. Lower handles until wrists are full hyperextended. Repeat.

name: sled one hand grip
comment: Stand in front of apparatus. Grasp bar grips with one hand; fingers around far bar and base of thumb around near stationary bar. Squeeze bar grips together until far bar makes contact with nearest bar. Release to original position and repeat.

name: sled one hand grip (plate loaded)
comment: Bend over and grasp bar grips with one hand; fingers under lower bar and base of thumb over top stationary bar. Squeeze bar grips together until lower bar makes contact with upper bar. Release to original position and repeat.

name: barbell reverse wrist curl
comment: Sit and grasp bar with narrow to shoulder width overhand grip. Rest forearms on thighs with wrists just beyond knees. Raise barbell by pointing knuckles upward as high as possible. Return until knuckles are pointing downward as far as possible. Repeat.

name: cable reverse wrist curl
comment: Sit and grasp cable bar with narrow to shoulder width overhand grip. Rest forearms on thighs with wrists just beyond knees. Raise stirrup by pointing knuckles upward as high as possible. Return until knuckles are pointing downward as far as possible. Repeat.

name: dumbbell reverse wrist curl
comment: Sit and grip dumbbell with overhand grip. Rest forearm on thigh with wrist just beyond knee. Raise dummbell by pointing knuckles upward as high as possible. Return until knuckles are pointing downward as far as possible. Repeat.

name: lever reverse wrist curl (plate loaded)
comment: Grasp bar with overhand grip. Place forearm on padded platform. Raise bar until wrist is fully hyperextended and return until wrist is fully flexed. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral reverse wrist curl
comment: Grasp handles with overhand grip to each side. Place forearms on arm pads. Raise handles until wrist is fully hyperextended. Lower handles until wrists are fully flexed. Repeat.

name: dumbbell seated pronation
comment: Sit next to elevated surface, approximately arm pit height. With hand of upper arm, grasp unilaterally loaded dumbbell; pinkie positioned near weighted side. Bend elbow approximately 90-degrees and place upperarm on elevated surface. Position thumb upward (supinated). Rotate dumbbell so thumb turns downward (pronation). Return and repeat.

name: lever isolateral seated forearm pronation
comment: Sit on seat. Grasp rotating handles with underhand grip on each side and place forearms on pads. Rotate shafts by turning palm downward. Return and repeat.

name: dumbbell seated supination
comment: Sit next to elevated surface, approximately arm pit height. With hand of arm next to elevated surface, grasp unilaterally loaded dumbbell; thumb next to side with weight. Bend elbow approximately 90-degrees and place upperarm on elevated surface. Position thumb downward (pronated). Rotate dumbbell so thumb turns upward (supination). Return and repeat.

name: lever isolateral forearm supination
comment: Sit on seat. Grasp rotating handles with overhand grip on each side and place forearms on pads. Rotate shafts by turning palm upward. Return and repeat.

name: barbell underhand bent-over row
comment: Bend knees slightly and bend over bar with back straight. Grasp bar with underhand grip. Pull bar to waist. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched downward. Repeat.

name: cable one arm straight back seated high row
aliases: cable one arm straight back seated high row
comment: Sit on seat or bench. Bend forward and grasp cable stirrup with one hand. Position torso upright allowing shoulder to be pulled forward under weight on cable. Pull cable attachment to side of torso while pulling shoulder back, arching spine, and pushing chest forward. Return until arm is extended and shoulder is pulled forward. Repeat. Continue with opposite arm.

name: cable straight back seated row
comment: Sit slightly forward on bench with feet on foot bar or vertical platform. Grasp close grip cable attachment. Straighten torso   upright and slide hips back so knees are slightly bent. Pull cable attachment to waist. Pull shoulders back and lift chest by arching back. Return until arms are extended, back is straight, and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: cable wide grip seated row
comment: Sit slightly forward on platform in order to grasp cable attachment with wider than shoulder width grip. Place feet on vertical platform and slide hips back positioning knees with slight bend. Pull cable attachment to waist while pulling torso upright.   pull shoulders back and push chest forward while arching back. Return until arms are extended, shoulders are stretched forward, and lower back is flexed forward. Repeat.

name: cable wide grip straight back seated row
aliases: cable wide grip straight back seated row
comment: Sit slightly forward on bench or platform in order to grasp cable bar attachment with wider than shoulder width overhand grip. Place feet on vertical platform. With torso upright, slide hips back positioning knees with slight bend. Pull cable attachment to waist. Pull shoulders back and lift chest by arching back. Return until arms are extended, back is straight, and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: dumbbell bent-over row
comment: Kneel over side of bench by placing knee and hand of supporting arm on bench. Position foot of opposite leg slightly back to side. Grasp dumbbell from floor. Pull dumbbell to up to side until it makes contact with ribs or until upper arm is just beyond horizontal. Return until arm is extended and shoulder is stretched downward. Repeat and continue with opposite arm.

name: dumbbell lying row
comment: Lie chest down on elevated bench. Grasp dumbbells below. Pull dumbbells up to sides. Arch thoracic spine, pull shoulder blades back, and continue to pull until upper arms are just beyond height of back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched downward. Repeat.

name: lever neutral grip incline row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever neutral grip incline row (plate loaded)
comment: Lie prone on inclined platform and place feet on foot rest. Grasp angled handles and lift lever out of support rack. Position lever directly under body with arms extended down. Pull lever up. As lever approaches, hyperextend thoracic spine and squeeze shoulders back. Lower lever until arms are extended and shoulders are pulled downward. Repeat.

name: lever wide grip incline row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever wide grip incline row (plate loaded)
comment: Lie prone on inclined platform and place feet on foot rest. Grasp wide handles and lift lever out of support rack. Position lever directly under body with arms extended down. Pull lever up. As lever approaches, hyperextend thoracic spine and squeeze shoulders back. Lower lever until arms are extended and shoulders are pulled downward. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral narrow grip seated row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral narrow grip seated row (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Pull levers back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral seated high row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral seated high row (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp lever handles with overhand grip. Pull lever back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral seated low row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral seated low row (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp lever handles with overhand/neutral grip. Pull levers back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral wide grip seated row (high bar, plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral wide grip seated row (high bar, plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Pull levers back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral wide grip seated row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever isolateral wide grip seated row (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Pull levers back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever close grip t-bar row (plate loaded)
aliases: lever close grip t-bar row (plate loaded)
comment: Bend knees slightly and bend over lever handles with back straight. Grasp narrow grip parallel lever handles. Pull handles up to waist. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched downward. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral seated high row
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp lever handles with overhand grip. Pull lever back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever seated row
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Push foot lever if available. Grasp narrower parallel grip handles. Pull lever back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever wide grip seated row
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp outer lever handles with overhand grip. Pull lever back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever seated row (no chest pad)
comment: Sit on seat and grasp handles with each hand. Place feet on vertically angled platform. Slide hips back with knees slightly bent. Pull handles to waist while straightening torso upright. Pull shoulders back and push chest forward while arching back. Return until arms are extended, shoulders are stretched forward, and lower back is flexed forward. Repeat.

name: lever straight back seated row (no chest pad)
aliases: lever straight back seated row (no chest pad)
comment:
Sit on seat with feet positioned on foot bar or platform.        Grasp handles with each hand. Position torso upright with knees        bent slightly.
pull handles to body. Pull shoulders back and lift chest        by arching back. Return until arms are extended, back is straight,        and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: lever underhand seated row
comment: Sit on seat and position chest against pad. Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Pull levers back until elbows are behind back and shoulders are pulled back. Return until arms are extended and shoulders are stretched forward. Repeat.

name: smith bent-over row
comment:
Bend knees slightly and bend over bar with back straight.        Grasp bar with wide overhand grip. Disengage bar by rotating        bar back.
pull bar to upper waist. Return until arms are extended and        shoulders are stretched downward. Repeat.

name: machine-assisted chin-up
comment: Step up and grasp bar with underhand shoulder width grip. Kneel onto platform or step onto bar. Pull body up until elbows are to sides. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: machine-assisted pull-up (open-centered bar, standing)
comment: Step up and grasp bar with wide overhand grip. Step down onto assistance lever or platform. Pull body up until neck reaches height of hands. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: machine-assisted parallel close grip pull-up
comment: Step up and grasp parallel grips. Kneel on padded platform and lower body down with arm extended. Pull body up until elbows are to sides. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: barbell pullover
comment: Lie upper back perpendicular on bench. Flex hips slightly. Grasp barbell from behind and position over chest with elbows bent slightly. With elbows bent slightly, lower bar over and beyond head until shoulders are fully flexed or upper arms are approximately parallel to torso. Return and repeat.

name: cable bent-over pullover
comment: Face high pulley and grasp revolving cable attachment with arm slightly bent. Place one foot slightly back and bend over at hip until shoulder is fully flexed (upper arms at sides of head). With elbows fixed approximately 30°, pull cable attachment down until upper arms are to sides. Return attachment overhead. Repeat.

name: cable parallel grip pulldown
comment: Grasp parallel cable attachment. Sit with thighs under supports. Pull down cable attachment to upper chest. Return until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable chin-up
comment: Place dip belt around waist. Kneel close to low pulley or lever and attach hook to dip belt chains. Step up and grasp bars with shoulder width underhand grip. Lift legs off of steps or floor. Pull body up until elbow are to sides. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable parallel close grip pull-up
comment: Place dip belt around waist. Kneel as close as possible to low pulley or lever and attach hook to dip belt in front between legs. Step up and grasp close grip p   arallel bars with hands facing inward. Lift legs off of steps or floor. Pull body up until elbow are to sides. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable parallel grip pull-up
comment: Place dip belt around waist. Kneel as close as possible to low pulley or lever and attach hook to dip belt in front between legs. Step up and grasp parallel bars with hands facing inward. Lift legs off of steps or floor. Pull body up until elbow are to sides. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable pull-up
comment: Place dip belt around waist. Kneel close to low pulley or lever and attach hook to dip belt. Step up and grasp bars with overhand wide grip. Lift legs off of steps or floor. Pull body up until chin is above bar. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: cable underhand pulldown
comment: Grasp cable bar with underhand grip. Sit with thighs under supports. Pull down cable bar to upper chest until elbows are to sides. Return until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral underhand pulldown (plate loaded)
comment: Grasp lever handles with underhand grip. Sit with thighs under supports. Pull down lever handles toward sides until elbows are to sides. Return until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever close grip pulldown
comment: Grasp parallel lever bars. Sit with thighs under supports. Pull down handles to sides of chest while leaning back slightly. Return until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever front pulldown
comment: Grasp lever handles. Sit with thighs under supports. Pull down lever to upper chest. Return until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever isolateral pulldown
comment: Sit on seat. Reach up and grasp handles with wide overhand grip. Pull levers down to sides of shoulders. Return until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: lever pullover
comment: Adjust seat height so lever is near shoulder axis. Sit on machine and push foot lever. Place elbows on pads and grasp bar from behind. Release foot lever and place feet on platform or to sides. Pull lever forward and down until elbows are to sides. Return until shoulders are fully flexed, or upper arms are parallel to torso. Repeat.

name: weighted parallel close grip pull-up
comment: Step up and grasp close grip parallel bars. Pull body up until elbow are to sides. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: band-assisted archer pull-up
comment: Loop either one band on center of bar as shown or two bands on each side of bar. Grab each side of hanging exercise band with both hands. Stretch exercise band down and step onto bottom inside of band with one foot. Stretch band down further by extending leg straight down. If second band is used, pull down and step into the other band. Reach up and grasp bar with overhand wide grip while keeping banded leg(s) straight. Pull body up to one side while keeping far arm extended or only slightly bent. Point elbow forward and downward while pulling body toward pulling hand. As chin approaches and rises above pulling hand, position extended arm by pointing elbow back while extending hand over top of bar. Lower body by extending bent arm while keeping extended arm straight and reestablishing grip on bar. When hanging from bar with both arms extended, repeat movement to opposite side. Continue alternating movement between sides.

name: archer pull-up
comment: Step up and grasp bar with overhand wide grip. Pull body up to one side while keeping far arm extended or only slightly bent. Point elbow forward and downward while pulling body toward pulling hand. As chin approaches and rises above pulling hand, position extended arm by pointing elbow back while extending hand over top of bar. Lower body by extending bent arm while keeping extended arm straight and reestablishing grip on bar. When hanging from bar with both arms extended, repeat movement to opposite side. Continue alternating movement between sides.

name: one arm pull-up
comment: Stand under bar with shoulder positioned 45° relative to bar angled toward working arm. Grasp bar with one hand using overhand shoulder width grip. Place other arm to side. Pull body up while turning shoulders inward perpendicular to bar. Elbow travels forward then down to navel while head is raised above bar on same side of bar as working arm. Near top of pull, shoulder of resting arm is pulled toward bar. Lower body until arm is fully extended in original starting position. Repeat.

name: partner-assisted chin-up
comment: Step up and grasp bar with underhand shoulder width grip. Bend knees so partner can assist from behind. Pull body up until elbows are to sides or chin is just above bar. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: partner-assisted pull-up
comment: Step up and grasp bar with wide overhand grip. Bend knees so partner can assist from behind. Pull body up until chin is just above bar. Lower body until arms and shoulders are fully extended. Repeat.

name: trap bar shrug
comment: Step into trap barbell and stand holding handles of trap bar to sides. Elevate shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: cable shrug
comment: Stand facing low pulley and grasp cable bar with shoulder width or slightly wider overhand grip. Stand close to pulley. With arms straight, elevate shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: cable isolateral shrug
comment: Stand between two low pulleys and grasp stirrups to each side. Stand upright with arms straight down to each side. With arms straight, elevate shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: dumbbell shrug
comment: Stand holding dumbbells to sides. Elevate shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: lever seated shrug (plate loaded)
comment: Sit on bench and grasp lower handles to each side. Sit upright. Elevate shoulders as high as possible. Lower and repeat.

name: lever shrug
